title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find when the vehicles are closest, consider their positions relative to the intersection over time.

title: Position of the Truck
text: The truck is moving east at $$80$$ $$\frac{km}{h}$$. Its position relative to the intersection after time $$t$$ is given by the equation: $$position_{truck}=32-80t$$.

title: Position of the Car
text: The car is moving north at $$50$$ $$\frac{km}{h}$$. Its position relative to the intersection after time $$t$$ is given by the equation: $$position_{car}=50t$$.

title: Distance Between the Vehicles
text: The distance between the truck and the car can be found using the Pythagorean theorem: $$distance=\sqrt{\left(32-80t\right)^2+\left(50t\right)^2}$$.

title: Minimizing the Distance
text: To find when the vehicles are closest, minimize the distance function with respect to time $$t$$.

title: Derivative of the Distance Function
text: Find the derivative of the distance function with respect to time $$t$$ and set it to zero to find the critical points.
